original_problem: In a small town, there are three water tanks: A, B, and C. Tank A can hold 1000 liters, Tank B can hold 800 liters, and Tank C can hold 1200 liters. The town's water supply system has three pipes: Pipe 1 connects Tank A to Tank B, Pipe 2 connects Tank B to Tank C, and Pipe 3 connects Tank A to Tank C. Pipe 1 can transfer water at a rate of 20 liters per minute, Pipe 2 at 15 liters per minute, and Pipe 3 at 30 liters per minute. If Tank A is filled to its maximum capacity and Tank B and Tank C are empty, how many minutes will it take to fill Tank C to its maximum capacity, given that Pipe 1 and Pipe 2 are always operating, but Pipe 3 only operates when Tank B is more than half full?
deconstructed_problem: In a small town, there are three water tanks: A, B, and C. Tank A can hold 1000 liters, Tank B can hold 800 liters, and Tank C can hold 1200 liters. The town's water supply system has three pipes: Pipe 1 connects Tank A to Tank B, Pipe 2 connects Tank B to Tank C, and Pipe 3 connects Tank A to Tank C. Pipe 1 can transfer water at a rate of 20 liters per minute, Pipe 2 at 15 liters per minute, and Pipe 3 at 30 liters per minute. ଯଦି (if) Tank A is filled to its maximum capacity and Tank B and Tank C are empty, how many minutes will it take to fill Tank C to its maximum capacity, given that Pipe 1 and Pipe 2 are always operating, but Pipe 3 only operates when Tank B is more than half full?
original_steps: Step 1: Calculate the initial rate at which Tank B is filled by Pipe 1 from Tank A.
Step 2: Determine when Tank B will be more than half full to activate Pipe 3 and calculate the combined rate of Pipes 1 and 3 filling Tank B and the rate of Pipe 2 filling Tank C from Tank B.
Step 3: Calculate the rate at which Tank C is filled once Pipe 3 is activated, considering the flow from both Pipe 2 (from Tank B) and Pipe 3 (directly from Tank A).
Step 4: Determine the time it takes for Tank C to fill to its maximum capacity of 1200 liters with the combined flow from Pipes 2 and 3.
Step 5: Account for the time before Pipe 3 is activated and calculate the total time required.
skeleton_steps: ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 1: Calculate the initial rate at which Tank B is filled by Pipe 1 from Tank A.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 2: Determine when Tank B will be more than half full to activate Pipe 3 and calculate the combined rate of Pipes 1 and 3 filling Tank B and the rate of Pipe 2 filling Tank C from Tank B.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 3: Calculate the rate at which Tank C is filled once Pipe 3 is activated, considering the flow from both Pipe 2 (from Tank B) and Pipe 3 (directly from Tank A).
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 4: Determine the time it takes for Tank C to fill to its maximum capacity of 1200 liters with the combined flow from Pipes 2 and 3.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 5: Account for the time before Pipe 3 is activated and calculate the ମୋଟ (total) time required.
